'''Kaide Ellis''' (born 4 August 1996) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} and {{rlp|lf}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Club career==
===Youth career===
[[File:Kaide Ellis.jpg|right|thumb|Ellis playing for the Penrith Panthers in 2018]]

Ellis was born in [[Dubbo, New South Wales]], [[Australia]], and was educated at [[Dubbo College]] Senior Campus where he represented 2013 NSW CHS team.

Kaide played his junior rugby league at [[Group 11 Rugby League|St Johns Dubbo]] and [[Group 11 Rugby League|South Dubbo Raiders]].

He signed with the [[Penrith Panthers]] and won [[2015 NRL Under-20s season|2015 National Youth Competition Grand Final]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Penrith Panthers===
Ellis made his first grade debut in Round 6 of the [[2018 NRL season]] against the [[Gold Coast Titans]] at [[Penrith Park]] which ended in a 35–12 victory.  Ellis was sin binned during the match for repeated infringements during the second half.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2018-04-10 |title=NRL teams round 6, 2018 |work=Daily Telegraph |url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/supercoach/nrl-teams-for-round-6-2018/news-story/1ab857b118eb59c4ca74f13845c841f2 |access-date=2018-04-10}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2018-06-08 |title='Embarrassment to the game' - Kaide Ellis sin-binning raises eyebrows in eventful Raiders-Panthers clash |url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/canberra-raiders-penrith-panthers-nrl-sin-bin-kaide-ellis-james-maloney-henry-perenara/zyk1hlvi6crt1mrccbo74auu3/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190809134356/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/canberra-raiders-penrith-panthers-nrl-sin-bin-kaide-ellis-james-maloney-henry-perenara/zyk1hlvi6crt1mrccbo74auu3 |archive-date=2019-08-09 |access-date=2019-08-09 |website=Sporting News}}</ref>

===St George Illawarra Dragons===
[[File:KAIDE ELLIS.jpg|thumb|upright|Ellis playing for the St. George Illawarra Dragons in 2021]]

On 12 July, Ellis was released by the Penrith club, signing for [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]] until the end of the [[2020 NRL season|2020 season]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2020-07-12 |title=Ellis granted release by Panthers |url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2020/07/12/ellis-granted-release-by-panthers/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200712110301/https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2020/07/12/ellis-granted-release-by-panthers/ |archive-date=2020-07-12 |access-date=2020-07-12 |website=Penrith Panthers}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Carayannis |first=Michael |date=2020-07-11 |title=Dragons relief after snapping up Graham replacement |work=The West Australian |url=https://thewest.com.au/sport/st-george-illawarra-dragons/st-george-illawarra-sign-penriths-kaide-ellis-as-replacement-for-james-graham-for-rest-of-season-ng-40b53ba9a5bf925b3c966daf31384209 |url-access=subscription |access-date=2020-07-12}}</ref>

In November 2020, he signed a one-year contract extension to remain at the club in 2021.<ref>{{Cite web |last1=St John |first1=Mark |last2=McDonald |first2=Darcie |date=2020-11-20 |title=NRL Transfer Centre: Rabbitohs release gun to Warriors, Johns signs with Knights |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-transfer-centre-james-roberts-wests-tigers-contract-south-sydney-rabbitohs/news-story/0163e57177a9d21cae3f15ac25d378ca?fbclid=IwAR1ygZnD0xpBAY5TXoWJ6X61gEcaVCwDmNuxsDJ7YKQvzxFdCYRi41mObSk/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201120171014/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-transfer-centre-james-roberts-wests-tigers-contract-south-sydney-rabbitohs/news-story/0163e57177a9d21cae3f15ac25d378ca |archive-date=2020-11-20 |access-date=2020-11-20 |website=Fox Sports}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors===
On 15 July 2021, Ellis signed for [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] on a three-year deal for the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Manchester Evening News|date=15 Jul 2021|accessdate=15 Jul 2021|title=Wigan Warriors 'sign' two NRL forwards for 2022|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-mago-kaide-ellis-21059254}}</ref>

In round 5 of the 2022 Super League season, Ellis was placed on report for fighting during the Wigan's 28–0 loss to [[Catalans Dragons]].  Ellis was subsequently banned for five matches over the incident.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://uk.sports.yahoo.com/news/kaide-ellis-jordan-dezaria-handed-200600818.html/|title=Kaide Ellis and Jordan Dezaria handed suspensions following tribunal|website=uk.sports.yahoo.com}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, he played for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In the 2023 Challenge Cup quarter-final, Ellis was sent off during Wigan's 14–12 victory over Warrington. On 20 June, Ellis was given a three-game ban and a £250 fine over the incident.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65953290|title=Josh Griffin: Hull FC centre gets seven-game ban after red card in Challenge Cup loss|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 14 October, Ellis played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

On 24 February, Ellis played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June, Ellis played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October, Ellis played in Wigan's 9–2 2024 Super League grand final victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

At the beginning of 2025, Ellis was praised for his strong start to the campaign, becoming a key member of [[Matt Peet]]'s squad.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors/wigan-warriors-the-remarkable-stats-which-underline-kaide-ellis-strong-start-to-2025-5026489|title=The remarkable stats which underline Kaide Ellis' strong start to 2025|date=10 March 2025|website=Wigan Today}}</ref> In April, he signed a new four-year deal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/articles/cp31qqn6glno|title=Wigan Warriors prop Kaide Ellis agrees new four-year contract|date=16 April 2025|website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Ellis again received public praise from Peet at the end of the season, praising his maturity and game development, with Peet considering him the club vice-captain.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/how-ever-present-kaide-ellis-evolved-into-wigan-warriors-leader-after-wake-up-call-5349002|title=How ever-present Ellis evolved into Warriors leader after wake-up call|date=7 October 2025|website=Wigan Today}}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Ellis played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 30 May 2026, Ellis played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship/title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

==Controversy==
On 5 July 2021, Ellis was fined $5000 by the [[NRL]] and suspended for one game after breaching the game's [[COVID-19]] biosecurity protocols when he  attended a party along with 12 other [[St. George Illawarra]] players at [[Paul Vaughan (rugby league)|Paul Vaughan]]'s property.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-07-05 |title=St George Illawarra Dragons players fined $305,000 and given match suspensions |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/05/st-george-illawarra-dragons-players-fined-$305000-and-given-match-suspensions/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210706031410/https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/07/05/st-george-illawarra-dragons-players-fined-$305000-and-given-match-suspensions/ |archive-date=2021-07-06 |access-date=2021-07-08 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref>
